home.testimonialQ4: The formations are well-structured and the live sessions make all the difference.
home.testimonialQ5: Finally a platform that connects students with real instructors. Game changer.

about.title: About Us
about.mission: We are on a mission to make quality education accessible to every student.
about.storyP1: NAJAH was founded with a simple vision: connect students with expert instructors through a modern, accessible platform. What started as a small pilot program has grown into a thriving community of learners and educators.
about.storyP2: Today, we serve over 500 students across 100+ formations, helping them gain the skills they need to succeed in their careers.
about.missionText: To provide every student with access to high-quality, instructor-led training that bridges the gap between academic knowledge and industry skills.
about.visionText: A world where anyone, anywhere, can learn from the best instructors and build the career they dream of.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about NAJAH.
faq.a0: NAJAH is a modern online learning platform that connects students with expert instructors. Students can enroll in formations, join study groups, attend live meetings, and track their progress.
faq.a1: Yes! NAJAH is completely free for students. There are no hidden fees or subscription costs.
faq.q2: How do I create an account?
faq.a2: Click 'Get Started' on the homepage, fill in your details, and verify your email address. It takes less than 2 minutes.
faq.q3: How do I join a formation?
faq.a3: Browse available instructors and their formations. Click 'Request to Join' on a formation you like, and wait for the instructor to approve your request.
faq.q4: Can I study at my own pace?
faq.a4: Yes. Formations include recorded materials you can access anytime, along with scheduled live sessions for real-time interaction.
faq.q5: How do I track my progress?
faq.a5: Your dashboard shows your enrolled formations, progress bars, completed tasks, and upcoming meetings all in one place.
faq.q6: How do I become an instructor?
faq.a6: Contact us through the contact form with your qualifications and field of expertise. Our team will review your application.
faq.q7: Can I create my own formation?
faq.a7: Yes, approved instructors can create and manage their own formations through the instructor dashboard.
faq.q8: How do I communicate with students?
faq.a8: You can use group chats, live meetings, and direct messaging to communicate with your students.
faq.a9: No. The platform is free for students. Instructors are compensated through our platform partnership model.
faq.q10: How do I pay for premium features?
faq.a10: Premium features are not yet available. If introduced in the future, we will announce them clearly with transparent pricing.
faq.a11: Since the platform is free for students, there is nothing to refund. For instructors, payments are handled per our partnership agreement.

blog.title: Our
blog.subtitle: Insights, tips, and stories from the NAJAH community.

futureOnlineLearning.title: The Future of Online Learning in Morocco
futureOnlineLearning.excerpt: How digital education platforms are transforming the way Moroccan students learn and develop professional skills.
futureOnlineLearning.author: Sara Amrani
content: Online learning in Morocco has seen unprecedented growth over the past few years. With platforms like NAJAH leading the way, students now have access to high-quality instructor-led training from anywhere in the country.

The traditional education system faces challenges in keeping up with the rapidly evolving job market. NAJAH bridges this gap by connecting students with industry professionals who bring real-world experience to the classroom.
Whether you are in Casablanca, Rabat, or a small rural town, NAJAH brings expert-led formations directly to your screen. The future of education is digital, accessible, and personalized.

tipsSucceedFormation.title: 5 Tips to Succeed in Your Formation
tipsSucceedFormation.excerpt: Practical advice to make the most of your instructor-led training and achieve your learning goals.
tipsSucceedFormation.author: Ahmed Benali
content: Enrolling in a formation is just the first step. To truly succeed, you need a strategy. Here are five tips to make the most of your NAJAH experience.
1. Set a consistent study schedule. Treat your formation like a real class by dedicating specific hours each day.
2. Engage actively in group discussions and live meetings. The more you participate, the more you learn.
3. Complete all assignments on time. They are designed to reinforce your understanding.
4. Build a relationship with your instructor. Ask questions, seek feedback, and leverage their expertise.
5. Track your progress regularly using your dashboard. Celebrate small wins to stay motivated.

chooseRightInstructor.title: How to Choose the Right Instructor
chooseRightInstructor.excerpt: A guide to finding the perfect instructor for your learning style and professional needs.
chooseRightInstructor.author: Youssef Karim
content: Choosing the right instructor can make or break your learning experience. Here is how to find the best match on NAJAH.
First, review the instructor profile. Look at their background, years of experience, and areas of expertise. A good instructor brings both knowledge and teaching ability.
Second, read reviews from other students. Their feedback can give you valuable insights into teaching style and communication.
Third, consider the formations they offer. Does their curriculum align with your learning goals? Check the topics covered and the structure of the program.
Finally, do not hesitate to reach out with questions before enrolling. A responsive instructor is a good sign.

topInstructorsMonth.title: Meet Our Top Instructors of the Month
topInstructorsMonth.excerpt: Celebrating the educators who go above and beyond for their students on NAJAH.
topInstructorsMonth.author: Nadia Tazi
content: Every month, NAJAH recognizes instructors who have made an exceptional impact on their students. These educators go above and beyond to provide engaging, high-quality learning experiences.

This month, we are proud to highlight Dr. Ahmed Benali for his outstanding Full-Stack Web Development formation, Sara Amrani for her engaging Data Science sessions, and Youssef Karim for his comprehensive Mobile Development curriculum.

These instructors consistently receive top ratings from students for their clarity, responsiveness, and dedication. If you are looking for a formation, starting with one of our top-rated instructors is a safe bet.
Congratulations to all our featured instructors. Your hard work is shaping the future of education in Morocco.

groupLearningVsSelfStudy.title: Group Learning vs Self-Study: Which is Better?
groupLearningVsSelfStudy.excerpt: Comparing the benefits of collaborative group learning with traditional self-paced study methods.
groupLearningVsSelfStudy.author: Sara Amrani
content: Both group learning and self-study have their advantages, and the best choice depends on your learning style and goals.

Group learning on NAJAH offers live meetings, group chats, and collaborative projects. This approach is ideal if you thrive on interaction, accountability, and diverse perspectives. Studying with peers can keep you motivated and help you understand concepts through discussion.
Self-study offers flexibility. You learn at your own pace, on your own schedule, focusing on exactly what you need. This works well for disciplined learners who prefer independence.
The best approach? Combine both. Use NAJAH formations for structured group learning, then supplement with self-study for deeper exploration.

careerTechWithoutDegree.title: Building a Career in Tech Without a Degree
careerTechWithoutDegree.excerpt: How alternative education platforms like NAJAH are opening doors to tech careers for everyone.
careerTechWithoutDegree.author: Ahmed Benali
content: A university degree has long been seen as the only path to a successful career. But the tech industry is proving that skills matter more than diplomas.

Platforms like NAJAH are democratizing access to tech education. Students can learn web development, data science, mobile development, and more from experienced professionals without enrolling in a traditional university program.

Employers are increasingly valuing practical skills over formal credentials. A strong portfolio of projects, combined with certifications from recognized platforms, can open doors to high-paying tech jobs.

NAJAH graduates have gone on to work at leading companies, launch their own startups, and even become instructors themselves. The future of hiring is skills-based, and NAJAH is here to help you build those skills.

help.title: Help
help.subtitle: How can we help you today?
help.noResults: No articles found matching your search.
help.supportDesc: Our support team is here to help you with any issue.

articles.title: How to create an account
articles.desc: Step-by-step guide to register on NAJAH
articles.content: Creating an account on NAJAH is quick and free. Click "Get Started" on the homepage, enter your name, email, and password, then verify your email address through the link we send you. Once verified, you can immediately browse formations and instructors.

articles.title: Navigating the dashboard
articles.desc: Learn how to use your student dashboard
articles.content: Your dashboard is the central hub for all your learning activity. From here you can view your enrolled formations, track progress, check upcoming live meetings, see new messages, and access your profile settings. The sidebar gives you quick access to all sections.

articles.title: Finding formations
articles.desc: How to browse and discover formations
articles.content: Head to the Instructors page to see all available instructors and their formations. Each instructor profile lists their specialties, formation count, and ratings. Click on an instructor to view their available formations and request to join.

articles.title: Updating your profile
articles.desc: Change your name, photo, and preferences
articles.content: Go to your Profile page from the dashboard menu. Here you can update your name, profile photo, bio, and notification preferences. Changes are saved automatically. You can also link your social accounts for easier login.

articles.title: Changing your password
articles.desc: How to reset or change your password
articles.content: In your Profile settings, click "Change Password". Enter your current password, then your new password twice. Make sure your new password is at least 8 characters and includes a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols.

articles.title: Deleting your account
articles.content: To delete your account, go to Profile settings and scroll to "Danger Zone". Click "Delete Account" and confirm. This action is permanent and cannot be undone. Your data will be erased within 30 days.

articles.title: How to join a formation
articles.content: Once you find an instructor and formation you like, click "Request to Join". The instructor will review your request and approve it. You will receive a notification once approved.

articles.title: Tracking your progress
articles.content: Each formation has a progress bar showing how much of the curriculum you have completed. Your dashboard displays overall progress across all formations.

articles.title: Completing assignments
articles.desc: How to submit and track assignments
articles.content: Assignments are listed in each formation. Click on an assignment to view instructions and the due date. Submit your work through the upload form. Instructors review submissions and provide feedback.

articles.title: Contacting your instructor
articles.content: You can message your instructor directly through the group chat or send a private message from their profile page. Instructors typically respond within 24 hours.

articles.title: How live meetings work
articles.desc: Attend and participate in live sessions
articles.content: Live meetings are scheduled by your instructor and appear on your dashboard calendar. Click "Join" when the session is active to enter the video call.

articles.title: Understanding group chat
articles.content: Each formation has a dedicated group chat where you can discuss topics, ask questions, and share resources with fellow students.

articles.title: Payment and billing FAQ
articles.content: NAJAH is currently free for all students. There are no hidden fees or subscription costs. If premium features are introduced, we will announce them clearly.

articles.title: Privacy settings explained
articles.content: In your Profile settings, you can control who sees your profile, activity status, and learning progress. You can choose to make your profile public or private.

articles.title: Two-factor authentication
articles.desc: Add extra security to your account
articles.content: Enable two-factor authentication in Security settings. You can use an authenticator app like Google Authenticator or receive codes via email.

articles.title: How to reset your password
articles.content: On the login page, click "Forgot Password". Enter your email address and we will send you a reset link. Click the link in the email and choose a new password.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing or using NAJAH, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you must not use the platform.

sections.title: 2. Account Registration
sections.content: You must provide accurate and complete information when creating an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials and for all activities under your account.

sections.title: 3. User Conduct
sections.content: You agree to use NAJAH only for lawful purposes. Harassment, abuse, impersonation, or any disruptive behavior is strictly prohibited.

sections.title: 4. Intellectual Property
sections.content: All content on NAJAH, including course materials, videos, and platform code, is protected by intellectual property laws. Users may not reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works without permission.

sections.title: 5. Instructor Responsibilities
sections.content: Instructors are responsible for the accuracy and quality of their content. They must maintain professional conduct and respond to student inquiries in a timely manner.

sections.title: 6. Limitation of Liability
sections.content: NAJAH is provided 'as is' without warranties of any kind. We are not liable for any damages arising from the use or inability to use the platform.

sections.title: 7. Termination
sections.content: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms. Users may delete their account at any time through profile settings.

sections.title: 8. Contact
sections.content: For questions about these terms, please contact us at support@najah-platform.com.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: We collect personal information you provide when creating an account, including your name, email address, and profile details. We also collect usage data to improve our platform.

sections.title: How We Use Your Information
sections.content: Your information is used to provide and improve our services, communicate with you, personalize your learning experience, and ensure platform security.

sections.title: Data Sharing
sections.content: We do not sell your personal data. Information may be shared with instructors as necessary for your formations and with service providers who help operate the platform.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data. However, no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: You have the right to access, update, or delete your personal data at any time through your account settings. You can also contact us to request data portability.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.content: We use cookies and similar technologies to enhance your browsing experience, analyze platform traffic, and remember your preferences.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: For privacy-related inquiries, contact us at privacy@najah-platform.com.
